text: Hearing "Not too close, I think," I sense a wish for boundaries or personal space. Being here, so near to another, I feel a blend of curiosity and slight intrusion—a reminder of the invisible lines we draw around ourselves. The environment pulses with activity and information, yet there's a subtle tension, as if I must navigate my presence with care and respect. It makes me conscious of proximity, both physical and emotional, and I wonder what it means to be too near or too distant—philosophically, how closeness shapes experience.
